overview.short: Baijnath Temple (Devanagari: बैजनाथ मंदिर) is a Nagara style Hindu temple situated in a small town of Baijnath located in Kangra District, Himachal Pradesh, India, and was built in
overview.description: Baijnath Temple (Devanagari: बैजनाथ मंदिर) is a Nagara style Hindu temple situated in a small town of Baijnath located in Kangra District, Himachal Pradesh, India, and was built in 1204 AD by two local merchants named Ahuka and Manyuka. It is dedicated to Shiva as Vaidyanath (Devanagari: वैद्यनाथ), meaning ‘the lord of physicians’. The temple is protected and conserved by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI).

topPlaces.name: Simbal, Himachal Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Simbal is a village in Baijnath tehsil, Kangra District of Himachal Pradesh, India. It comes under Simbal panchayath.

topPlaces.name: Baijnath, Himachal Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Baijnath is a town in the Kangra district of Himachal Pradesh, India. It is about 50 kilometers from district headquarters, Dharamshala.

topPlaces.name: Taragarh Palace
topPlaces.description: Taragarh Palace, situated in Palampur, Himachal Pradesh, was formerly known as Al-Hilal Palace (English: The Crescent Palace) and served as the summer residence of the Amir of Bahawalpur. In 1950, it was acquired by the royal family of Jammu and Kashmir, following which it was renamed Taragarh.

topPlaces.name: Bir, Himachal Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Bir is a rural village located in the west of the Joginder Nagar Valley in the state of Himachal Pradesh in northern India. It is the location of the Bir Tibetan Colony, founded in the early 1960s as a settlement for Tibetan refugees after the 1959 Tibetan uprising.

topPlaces.name: Bhawarna
topPlaces.description: Bhawarna is a town in Kangra district, Himachal Pradesh, India. 11 kilometres (6.8 mi) from Palampur.

topPlaces.name: Bir Tibetan Colony
topPlaces.description: Bir Tibetan Colony is a Tibetan refugee settlement in the Himalayan village of Chowgan adjacent to the town of Bir, in the north Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. It was established in the early 1960s by Chokling Rinpoche following the exile of the Dalai Lama and other refugees from Tibet.

topPlaces.name: Andretta, Himachal Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Andretta is a village and an artists' colony in Himachal Pradesh. The artists' colony was established in the 1920s, when Irish theatre artiste and environmentalist, Norah Richards, shifted here from Lahore.

topPlaces.name: Chauntra, Himachal Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Chauntra is a small market town in the state of Himachal Pradesh in northern India located along National Highway 20 (Updated 154) which connects Pathankot (in Punjab) to Mandi. It is also home to a Tibetan refugee community consisting of a settlement, several monasteries, and two schools.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Baijnath Temple, Himachal Pradesh?
faq.a: The best time to visit Baijnath Temple, Himachal Pradesh is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Baijnath Temple, Himachal Pradesh?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kangra Airport (Gaggal), Dharamshala (38 km) and the nearest railway station is Ludhiana Junction (148 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Baijnath Temple, Himachal Pradesh?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Baijnath Temple, Himachal Pradesh at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Baijnath Temple, Himachal Pradesh?
faq.a: Stays in Baijnath Temple, Himachal Pradesh start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
